title: One year ahead of French presidential election, survey shows deep desire for change
subhead: Following the municipal elections, the political landscape is marked by a favorable perception of the far-right Rassemblement National and by highly polarized preferences among French voters, a Le Monde survey shows.

French court condemns 'organized, opaque and illegal' terrorist funding system in Lafarge trial
subhead: During the ruling, the president of the court drew an explicit connection between the 'commercial partnership' that the French cement company had entered with terrorist groups and the attacks that were then carried out by those groups.

title: Reza Pahlavi's controversial visit to Sweden's Parliament
subhead: The eldest son of Iran's last shah was received by MPs in Stockholm on Monday. His visit sparked excitement among some Iranians in exile, but also anger from those who do not recognize his legitimacy.

title: Will California's next governor be a Republican?
subhead: The crowded field of Democratic candidates running in the gubernatorial election raises the possibility that a Trump-aligned Republican could win in November in this deeply progressive state.

title: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
subhead: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

Pope embarks on African tour, a strategic continent for Catholicism
subhead: From April 13 to 23, Pope Léon XIV is set to visit Algeria, Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea. The trip symbolizes the shift in the center of gravity of the Catholic Church, whose future now lies in Africa.

title: Hungary celebrates 'return to Europe' after Orban's crushing electoral defeat
subhead: Péter Magyar's party, Tisza, won more than 53% of the vote in the legislative elections, far ahead of the prime minister's Fidesz, which managed barely 38%. Tisza is expected to secure 138 out of the 199 seats in Parliament, giving it a constitutional supermajority.

title: Chambord, the second-most visited château in France, strives to fund much-needed renovation
subhead: The director of the Loire Valley estate, Pierre Dubreuil, has launched a large-scale crowdfunding campaign aimed at the general public in order to complete the renovation budget.
